review_pass: phase-4 focused plan-review after Phases 1-3 were reported complete
patch_pass: phase-4 blocker and warning remediation applied to the Phase 4 plan document and synchronized overview text
summary: Phase 4 review returned CHANGES_REQUESTED for the deferred sweep hook coverage, stale resume fixture reuse, non-executable test snippets, narrow durability coverage, underpowered exact-witness negatives, missing AST inventory gate, and stale RED/import wording. This patch updates Phase 4 and the overview to add the public resume no-work terminalization sweep, replace stale RowOutcome-based resume fixture reuse with an ADR-019-compatible helper, fix the I1a/I3/coalesce snippets, add exact sink-node and artifact witness negatives, expand the durability matrix to fresh/resume/no-op I1a/I1b plus I1c/I3 real-time producer crash rows, require exact evidence assertions, and include the ADR-019 AST symbol inventory gate. The plan is patched, not approved: run another plan-review pass before executing Phase 4.

patched_blockers.title: No-op resume branch bypasses deferred invariant sweep
changes: Phase 4 goal, file list, Task 4.3 hook instructions, DoD, and commit message now require a sweep in the public resume no-work terminalization branch before terminal status derivation/finalization.
The no-work branch instructions require sweep failures to route through the failed ceremony instead of successful terminalization.

Durability tests now include parametrized no-op resume I1a and I1b cases that assert _process_resumed_rows is not called, the sweep is called, the run finalizes FAILED, and planted evidence remains queryable.

patched_blockers.title: Resume durability fixture reused stale RowOutcome-only helper
changes: Phase 4 now forbids reusing TestResumeLifecycle._setup_failed_run unless it is first updated for ADR-019 two-axis outcomes.

The durability snippet defines _setup_adr019_failed_resume_run, preserving production graph/manual resume setup while recording processed rows with TerminalOutcome.SUCCESS and TerminalPath.DEFAULT_FLOW.
Resume tests now call the local ADR-019-compatible helper instead of importing TestResumeLifecycle.

patched_blockers.title: Concrete test snippets were not executable against current contracts
changes: I1a happy path no longer records a duplicate parent outcome after fork_token, which already writes the parent FORK_PARENT row.
I3 wrong sink name is now a scalar-guard ValueError test instead of expecting AuditIntegrityError from the cross-table layer.
The fork/coalesce false-positive regression now uses identity branches and coalesce on_success='default' instead of branch transforms wired to the default sink before coalesce.

patched_strong_warnings.title: Durability coverage did not cover every new Tier 1 invariant crash
changes: Task 4.4 matrix now covers fresh I1a, fresh I1b, resume I1a, resume I1b, no-op resume I1a, no-op resume I1b, I1c real-time producer crash, and I3 real-time producer crash.
Every matrix row requires propagation, FAILED finalization, and evidence preservation.
The RED/GREEN expectations and DoD now name no-op resume and real-time I1c/I3 durability rows explicitly.

patched_strong_warnings.title: I1c exact witness negatives were too weak
changes: Added failsink wrong-sink-node negative test to prove the recorder does not accept any completed sink state or infer from sink_name.
Added failsink wrong-artifact negative test to prove artifact_id must be the exact artifact for the declared run and failsink node.
I1c durability guidance now says to corrupt producer witness arguments rather than monkeypatching the invariant method directly.

patched_strong_warnings.title: Phase 4 final gate omitted ADR-019 AST symbol inventory
changes: Task 4.5 now runs scripts/cicd/adr019_symbol_inventory.py check with --root src/elspeth and --allowlist config/cicd/adr019_symbol_inventory.
The final DoD now requires the AST symbol inventory gate.

patched_strong_warnings.title: RED/import wording was stale after Phases 1-3
changes: Prerequisites now state Phases 1-3 are complete and Phase 4 must use the two-axis record_token_outcome signature.
The import sketch now imports NodeType from elspeth.contracts and keeps BatchStatus/TerminalOutcome/TerminalPath in elspeth.contracts.enums.
RED expectations now distinguish cross-table failures from scalar guard behaviour and happy-path documentation tests.

next_actions: Run a fresh plan-review pass focused on Phase 4 before implementing it.
If the next review passes, execute Phase 4 with Phases 1-3 already present and preserve the atomic Stage 2/3 merge boundary.
If the next review finds any executable-snippet drift, patch the exact snippets before implementation.
